title: freestiler
text: Rust-powered, MLT-first vector tile engine R. Creates PMTiles archives sf objects zero external dependencies (tippecanoe, Java, Go).

title: freestiler
text: freestiler takes sf data frame produces .pmtiles file containing vector tiles. supports two tile formats: MLT (MapLibre Tiles) — default. next-gen columnar binary format announced Jan 2026. one first MLT encoders outside reference Java/C++ implementations. MVT (Mapbox Vector Tiles) — protobuf-based, supported everywhere. Used compatibility fallback. entire pipeline runs -memory: sf → Rust (via extendr) → tile encoding → PMTiles archive. temp files, shelling .

title: freestiler
code: R: freestile(sf_obj, "output.pmtiles")   → .decompose_geometries() extracts flat coordinate vectors from sf   → .extract_properties() extracts typed attribute columns   → rust_freestile() calls into Rust via extendr  Rust:   lib.rs       — extendr entry point, parses R vectors into Feature structs   tiler.rs     — tile math (lon/lat ↔ tile x/y), assigns features to tiles per zoom   clip.rs      — clips geometries to tile boundaries (Cohen-Sutherland for lines, Sutherland-Hodgman for polygons)   simplify.rs  — tile-pixel grid snapping (prevents slivers, zoom-adaptive simplification)   mvt.rs       — MVT protobuf encoder (prost-based, hand-written structs matching vector_tile.proto)   mlt.rs       — MLT columnar binary encoder (varint, delta encoding, typed property streams)   pmtiles_writer.rs — assembles tiles into PMTiles v3 archive with gzip compression

title: freestiler
text: Build system: Copied spopt-r (/Users/kylewalker/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/dev/spopt-r). Uses configure/configure.win → tools/config.R pattern Makevars.placeholder replacement. CRAN-ready vendor tarball support. extendr 0.8: R ↔︎ Rust bridge. #[extendr] function rust_freestile receives flat vectors (coords, offsets, typed property columns) R. Geometry decomposition: R side (freestile.R) decomposes sf geometries flat coordinate arrays offset vectors rings/parts. Important: uses sf::st_geometry_type() get geometry types, class(g)[1] (returns “XY”). MLT tile_type patching: pmtiles2 crate doesn’t MLT (0x06) TileType enum, write MVT patch byte 99 PMTiles header 0x06 writing. Parallel tiling: Uses rayon two-level parallelism — across tiles within tiles (features processed parallel). Critical low-zoom tiles containing many features. Simplification: Uses tile-pixel grid snapping instead Douglas-Peucker. Coordinates rounded tile’s 4096×4096 pixel grid consecutive duplicates removed. prevents slivers adjacent polygons (shared vertices snap pixel) provides natural zoom-adaptive simplification. Polygon clipping: Sutherland-Hodgman O(4n) rectangle clipping, bbox fast-paths fully-contained fully-outside polygons. Replaced original geo::BooleanOps intersection O((n+k)log n). Tiny feature dropping: Sub-pixel features (bbox < 1 pixel current zoom) skipped lower zoom levels, dramatically reducing work large datasets.

title: freestiler
code: # From within the freestiler directory: NOT_CRAN=true devtools::load_all() devtools::test()  # Quick manual test: library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf")) freestile(nc, "test.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") freestile(nc, "test_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mvt")

title: freestiler
code: freestile(   input,                    # sf object   output,                   # Output .pmtiles path   layer_name = NULL,        # Layer name (derived from filename if NULL)   tile_format = "mlt",      # "mlt" (default) or "mvt"   min_zoom = 0,   max_zoom = 14,   simplification = TRUE,    # Grid-snap to tile pixels (prevents slivers)   generate_ids = TRUE,      # Sequential feature IDs   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: freestiler
text: MVP complete tests pass (10/10) Supports: POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON Property types: character, integer, numeric, logical (NA handling) Auto-transforms WGS84 input CRS differs PMTiles headers verified correct (tile_type=1 MVT, tile_type=6 MLT, version=3)

title: freestiler
text: Fix tile boundary seam artifacts (visible zoom 6-8; geometry verified correct adjacent tiles, winding correct, dropped features — root cause still investigation) base_zoom parameter: zoom level features stop dropped, ensuring features present client-side counting/querying (inspired tippecanoe’s --base-zoom / -B) Advanced MLT encodings (FastPFOR, FSST) Direct file input (GeoJSON, GeoPackage) Integration pmtiles package (pm_create(engine = "freestiler"))

title: Getting Started
text: freestiler Rust-powered vector tile engine R Python. takes sf data frames GeoDataFrames produces PMTiles archives – self-contained, single-file tilesets can served statically viewed locally. whole pipeline runs -process: tippecanoe, Java, Go. freestiler supports two tile formats: MLT (MapLibre Tiles) – default. next-generation columnar binary format efficient vector tile encoding. MVT (Mapbox Vector Tiles) – widely supported protobuf-based format. package designed two different use cases: small reproducible examples, want quick path spatial data PMTiles serious tiling jobs, data already lives DuckDB want stream directly SQL

title: Getting Started

Windows currently uses R duckdb fallback default GNU DuckDB toolchain issue sorted . install development version GitHub: Install PyPI: Published wheels include native feature set, including DuckDB-backed file input SQL query support. title: Getting Started
text: small examples vignette reproducible easy follow. API built handle much larger jobs. recent local run, freestile_query() streamed 146,575,672 US job points DuckDB MVT PMTiles archive 12 minutes, producing 2.3 GB output 978,589 tiles.
code: freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Getting Started
text: main function freestile(). Pass spatial data output path. R counties example intentionally simple. useful verifying installation works, interesting demonstration package can . Python
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") Creating MLT tiles (zoom 0-14) for 100 features across 1 layer...   Tiling layer 'counties' (zoom 0-14)... Created nc_counties.pmtiles (65.2 KB) import geopandas as gpd from freestiler import freestile  gdf = gpd.read_file("nc.shp")  freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties") Creating MLT tiles (zoom 0-14) for 100 features across 1 layer...   Tiling layer 'counties' (zoom 0-14)... Created nc_counties.pmtiles (65.2 KB)

title: Getting Started
text: Use mapgl package view tileset R. mapgl supports MLT MVT tiles. PMTiles need HTTP range requests, start local file server first: point mapgl URL: Python viewer stacks less settled R mapgl path. maximum compatibility today, create tiles tile_format="mvt": Viewer support MLT Python improve Python wrappers catch current MapLibre GL JS releases. meantime, MVT safe default Python-facing examples, either MVT MLT can viewed directly browser current MapLibre GL JS.
code: # Serve from /tmp on port 8082 (pick whichever you prefer) npx http-server /tmp -p 8082 --cors -c-1 library(mapgl)  maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "counties-src",     url = "http://localhost:8082/nc_counties.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "county-fill",     source = "counties-src",     source_layer = "counties",     fill_color = "#00897b",     fill_opacity = 0.5   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "county-borders",     source = "counties-src",     source_layer = "counties",     line_color = "#004d40",     line_width = 1   ) freestile(gdf, "nc_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name="counties", tile_format="mvt")

Use MVT maximum viewer compatibility needed – particularly Python viewers today large public-facing point tilesets.

title: Getting Started
text: min_zoom max_zoom parameters control zoom range. R Python
code: freestile(nc, "nc_z4_10.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 10 ) freestile(gdf, "nc_z4_10.pmtiles",     layer_name="counties",     min_zoom=4,     max_zoom=10 )

title: Getting Started
text: large datasets, drop_rate provides exponential feature thinning lower zoom levels. Points thinned using spatial ordering (Morton curve) maintain even coverage; polygons lines thinned area. base_zoom parameter controls zoom level features present. base_zoom, features progressively thinned. default, base_zoom equals max_zoom. R Python
code: freestile(nc, "nc_dropping.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   drop_rate = 2.5,   base_zoom = 10 ) freestile(gdf, "nc_dropping.pmtiles",     layer_name="counties",     drop_rate=2.5,     base_zoom=10 )

title: Getting Started
text: Tile spatial files directly without loading R Python first. R – supports GeoParquet, GeoPackage, Shapefile, formats via DuckDB: Python – published wheels include DuckDB engine, GeoPackage, Shapefile, DuckDB-backed formats work box:
code: # GeoParquet (uses the geoparquet engine) freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats (uses the DuckDB engine) freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb") from freestiler import freestile_file  # GeoParquet (uses the geoparquet engine) freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats (uses the DuckDB engine) freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine="duckdb")

title: Getting Started
text: Run SQL query DuckDB’s spatial extension pipe results directly tiling engine. Filter, join, transform data SQL tiling. R Python large point datasets, set streaming = "always" force streaming point pipeline:
code: freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp') WHERE pop > 50000",   "large_counties.pmtiles" )  freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",   "nc_blocks.pmtiles" ) from freestiler import freestile_query  freestile_query(     "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp') WHERE pop > 50000",     "large_counties.pmtiles" )  freestile_query(     "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",     "nc_blocks.pmtiles" ) freestile_query(   query = "SELECT category, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM points_table",   output = "points.pmtiles",   layer_name = "points",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always" )

title: Getting Started
text: Pass named list (R) dictionary (Python) create multi-layer tilesets. R Use freestile_layer() per-layer zoom control: Python – use freestile_layer() per-layer zoom control:
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(counties = nc, centroids = pts),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" ) freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" ) from freestiler import freestile, freestile_layer  centroids = gdf.copy() centroids.geometry = gdf.geometry.centroid  freestile(     {         "counties": freestile_layer(gdf, min_zoom=0, max_zoom=10),         "centroids": freestile_layer(centroids, min_zoom=6, max_zoom=14),     },     "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: Getting Started
text: point layers, cluster_distance merges nearby points clusters point_count attribute. R Python
code: freestile(pts, "nc_clustered.pmtiles",   layer_name = "centroids",   cluster_distance = 50,   cluster_maxzoom = 8 ) freestile(centroids, "nc_clustered.pmtiles",     layer_name="centroids",     cluster_distance=50,     cluster_maxzoom=8 )

title: Getting Started
text: coalesce parameter merges features identical attributes within tile. Lines sharing endpoints joined; polygons grouped MultiPolygons. R Python
code: freestile(nc, "nc_coalesced.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   coalesce = TRUE ) freestile(gdf, "nc_coalesced.pmtiles",     layer_name="counties",     coalesce=True )

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Vector tiles divide geographic data grid square tiles multiple zoom levels. Instead pre-rendering images (raster tiles), store actual geometry attribute data, allowing client-side styling, interaction, dynamic rendering. standard container vector tilesets PMTiles – single-file, cloud-native archive can served static file host.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) dominant vector tile encoding. Introduced Mapbox 2014, MVT uses Protocol Buffers (protobuf) serialize geometry attributes. supported every major mapping library: MapLibre GL, Mapbox GL, Leaflet, deck.gl, . MVT encodes geometry integer coordinates 4096x4096 pixel grid using relative move/line commands. Attributes stored per-feature using key-value tag system string interning.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MapLibre Tiles (MLT) columnar binary tile format announced January 2026 MapLibre organization. MVT stores data row--row (one feature time), MLT organizes data column--column – x-coordinates together, y-coordinates together, values given attribute together. columnar layout enables efficient compression techniques: Varint encoding – small integers use fewer bytes Delta encoding – coordinates stored differences previous values Run-length encoding (RLE) – repeated values compressed count + value Dictionary encoding – string columns unique values stored indices dictionary polygon-heavy datasets, MLT typically 10-20% smaller MVT: Point-datasets see minimal savings coordinate data dominates compresses similarly formats.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: polygon-heavy datasets, MLT typically 10-20% smaller MVT: Point-datasets see minimal savings coordinate data dominates compresses similarly formats.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: freestiler includes spec-compliant MLT encoder written Rust. encoding pipeline tile: Feature sorting – features sorted Morton curve (Z-order) within tile optimal delta encoding coordinates. Geometry streams – coordinates delta-encoded varint-compressed. Topology (ring lengths, part counts) uses integer RLE beneficial. Property streams – attribute column encoded independently: Integers: varint + delta encoding Floats: raw IEEE 754 Strings: dictionary encoding cardinality low, raw otherwise Booleans: byte-RLE ORC-style present streams Nullable handling – null/NA values produce PRESENT bitmap streams using byte-RLE encoding, matching ORC specification. encoder validated mlt-core 0.1.2 reference decoder.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Key performance features: Two-level parallelism – rayon processes tiles parallel features within tiles parallel. Tile-pixel grid snapping – instead Douglas-Peucker simplification, coordinates snap tile’s 4096x4096 pixel grid. Shared vertices adjacent polygons snap pixel, preventing slivers. Buffered assignment – features assigned tiles using expanded bounds (5% buffer) matching clipping buffer. eliminates seam artifacts tile boundaries. Sub-pixel fast paths – features smaller one pixel given zoom level skipped, dramatically reducing work low zooms.
code: sf / GeoDataFrame / file / SQL query     │     ▼ R or Python: decompose geometries + extract properties     │     ▼ Rust: assign features to tiles (buffered bounds)     │     ▼ Rust: per-tile (parallel via rayon)     ├── clip geometry to tile boundary     ├── snap to pixel grid (simplification)     ├── encode as MLT or MVT     └── gzip compress     │     ▼ Rust: assemble PMTiles v3 archive     │     ▼ .pmtiles file

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MLT new format (January 2026). Current ecosystem support: Encoding: freestiler (Rust, R + Python), mlt-core reference encoder (Java/C++) Decoding: MapLibre GL JS (experimental), mlt-core decoder Viewing: MapLibre GL JS MLT plugin maximum compatibility needed today, use tile_format = "mvt". MLT support expands across mapping libraries, default MLT format become directly viewable without conversion.

title: freestiler
text: freestiler creates PMTiles vector tilesets R Python. Pass sf object GeoDataFrame, file disk, DuckDB SQL query writes single .pmtiles file can serve anywhere. tiling engine written Rust runs entirely -process, ’s nothing else install. Tile format options MapLibre Tiles (MLT) Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT). features include multi-layer tilesets, point clustering, feature coalescing, exponential drop rates large datasets.

title: freestiler
text: R Python API, one Rust engine. tiler powers packages. Direct DuckDB tiling. Filter, join, transform data SQL writing tiles. PMTiles output. Write single portable archive instead tile directory tree. Serious point workloads. Large DuckDB point queries can stream directly PMTiles without loading full result memory.

title: freestiler
text: just small-data convenience wrapper around vector tile encoding. recent local run, freestile_query() streamed 146,575,672 US job points DuckDB MVT PMTiles archive 12 minutes, producing 2.3 GB tileset 978,589 tiles. want maximum viewer compatibility today, use tile_format = "mvt" large public-facing point tilesets.
code: freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: freestiler
text: Install r-universe: r-universe build recommended install serious DuckDB-backed workloads. Native macOS Linux builds include Rust DuckDB backend default, including streaming point pipeline used freestile_query(). Windows currently uses R duckdb fallback default GNU DuckDB toolchain issue sorted . install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages('freestiler', repos = c('https://walkerke.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("walkerke/freestiler")

title: freestiler
text: Install PyPI: PyPI wheels ship native feature set, including GeoPandas input, direct GeoParquet file input, DuckDB-backed file input, SQL query support. See Python Setup article venv setup optional features.
code: pip install freestiler

title: freestiler
text: R Python North Carolina counties good smoke test, big advantage freestiler API scales much larger jobs.
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") import geopandas as gpd from freestiler import freestile  gdf = gpd.read_file("nc.shp")  freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties")

title: freestiler
text: R – mapgl supports MLT MVT. PMTiles need HTTP range requests, start local server first (e.g. npx http-server /tmp -p 8082 --cors -c-1): Python – Python viewer stacks still catching MLT, use tile_format="mvt" now unless viewing browser current MapLibre GL JS. can also view PMTiles file (MLT MVT) browser MapLibre GL JS 5.17+ PMTiles protocol.
code: library(mapgl)  maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "counties-src",     url = "http://localhost:8082/nc_counties.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "county-fill",     source = "counties-src",     source_layer = "counties",     fill_color = "#00897b",     fill_opacity = 0.5   ) # Create MVT tiles for Python viewer compatibility freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties", tile_format="mvt")

title: freestiler
text: data already lives DuckDB, freestile_query() serious-work path. streaming matters. R Python
code: freestile_query(   query = "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",   output = "nc_blocks.pmtiles",   layer_name = "blocks" ) from freestiler import freestile_query  freestile_query(     query="SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",     output="nc_blocks.pmtiles",     layer_name="blocks", )

title: freestiler
text: can also tile files disk without reading memory first. R – supports GeoParquet, GeoPackage, Shapefile, formats via DuckDB: Python – published wheels include DuckDB engine, GeoPackage, Shapefile, DuckDB-backed formats work box:
code: freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb") from freestiler import freestile_file  freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine="duckdb")

title: freestiler
text: R Python
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" ) from freestiler import freestile, freestile_layer  centroids = gdf.copy() centroids.geometry = gdf.geometry.centroid  freestile(     {         "counties": freestile_layer(gdf, min_zoom=0, max_zoom=10),         "centroids": freestile_layer(centroids, min_zoom=6, max_zoom=14),     },     "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: freestiler
text: R Python
code: # Exponential drop rate: thin features at low zoom levels freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", drop_rate = 2.5, base_zoom = 10)  # Point clustering freestile(pts, "pts.pmtiles", cluster_distance = 50, cluster_maxzoom = 8)  # Coalesce features with identical attributes freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", coalesce = TRUE) freestile(gdf, "nc.pmtiles", drop_rate=2.5, base_zoom=10)  freestile(centroids, "pts.pmtiles", cluster_distance=50, cluster_maxzoom=8)  freestile(gdf, "nc.pmtiles", coalesce=True)

text: Getting Started – full tutorial R Python examples MapLibre Tiles (MLT) – freestiler encodes MLT format Python Setup – building source, optional features, viewer details

what: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: Creates PMTiles archive containing vector tiles one sf data frames. Supports Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) MapLibre Tile (MLT) formats, multi-layer output, feature dropping, point clustering, feature coalescing.

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: freestile(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   generate_ids = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: input sf data frame, named list sf/freestile_layer objects multi-layer output. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. used single-layer input. tile_format Character. Tile encoding format: "mlt" (default) MapLibre Tiles "mvt" Mapbox Vector Tiles. min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present (dropping). NULL (default) uses layer's max_zoom. drop-rate curve also computed relative base_zoom, lowering produces gentler thinning low zooms. Inspired tippecanoe's -B / --base-zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate feature thinning (e.g. 2.5). zoom level base_zoom, features retained rate 1/drop_rate^(base_zoom - zoom). Points thinned using spatial ordering; polygons/lines thinned area. NULL (default) disables drop-rate thinning. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance point clustering. Points within radius merged cluster features point_count attribute. NULL (default) disables clustering. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Maximum zoom level clustering. zoom, individual points shown. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes within tile (default FALSE). Lines sharing endpoints merged; polygons grouped MultiPolygons. simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid zoom level (default TRUE). provides zoom-adaptive simplification prevents slivers adjacent polygons. generate_ids Logical. Whether assign sequential feature IDs (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output file (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress messages (default FALSE).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  # Single layer freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties")  # Multi-layer pts <- st_centroid(nc) freestile(   list(counties = nc, centroids = pts),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )  # With dropping and coalescing freestile(nc, "nc_drop.pmtiles", drop_rate = 2.5, coalesce = TRUE)  # With point clustering freestile(pts, "pts.pmtiles", cluster_distance = 50, cluster_maxzoom = 8) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: Reads GeoParquet, GeoPackage, Shapefile, spatial file directly tiling engine. GeoParquet engine requires compilation FREESTILER_GEOPARQUET=true. DuckDB engine uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package (reads file via DuckDB's ST_Read(), auto-detects source CRS via ST_Read_Meta(), reprojects WGS84). Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: freestile_file(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   engine = "geoparquet" )

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: input Character. Path input spatial file. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mlt" (default) "mvt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). engine Character. Backend engine: "geoparquet" (default, GeoParquet files) "duckdb" (file format DuckDB supports).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ freestile_file("data.parquet", "output.pmtiles") freestile_file("data.gpkg", "output.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb") } # }

what: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: Wraps sf object optional per-layer zoom range overrides use multi-layer tile generation.

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
code: freestile_layer(input, min_zoom = NULL, max_zoom = NULL)

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: input sf data frame. min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level layer. NULL, uses global min_zoom freestile(). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level layer. NULL, uses global max_zoom freestile().

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: freestile_layer object (list class attribute).

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf")) roads <- st_read("roads.shp")  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     roads = freestile_layer(roads, min_zoom = 8, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "layers.pmtiles" ) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: Executes SQL query via DuckDB's spatial extension pipes results tiling engine. Uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package. Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: freestile_query(   query,   output,   db_path = NULL,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   source_crs = NULL,   streaming = "auto" )

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: query Character. SQL query returns geometry column. DuckDB spatial functions like ST_Read() read_parquet() available. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. db_path Character. Path DuckDB database file, NULL (default) -memory database. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mlt" (default) "mvt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). source_crs Character NULL. CRS geometry returned query, example "EPSG:4326" "EPSG:4267". Used R duckdb fallback; ignored Rust DuckDB backend. streaming Character. DuckDB query execution mode: "auto" (default), "always", "never".

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: using R fallback, source_crs must supplied explicitly query result can interpreted reprojected correctly. Pass "EPSG:4326" SQL already returns WGS84 geometry, source CRS string (example "EPSG:4267") DuckDB reproject WGS84 tiling. file-based input CRS embedded file, use freestile_file() engine = "duckdb" instead, auto-detects source CRS.

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Query a GeoParquet file freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('data.parquet') WHERE pop > 50000",   "output.pmtiles" )  # Query a Shapefile freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp')",   "counties.pmtiles" )  # Query with an existing DuckDB database freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE region = 'West'",   "west.pmtiles",   db_path = "my_database.duckdb" ) } # }

what: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Create vector tile archives PMTiles format sf spatial data frames. Supports Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) MapLibre Tile (MLT) output formats. Uses Rust backend via 'extendr' fast, -memory tiling zero external system dependencies.

title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Maintainer: Kyle Walker kyle@walker-data.com

what: Create vector tiles from spatial data (multi-layer support) — rust_freestile
title: Create vector tiles from spatial data (multi-layer support) — rust_freestile
text: Create vector tiles spatial data (multi-layer support)

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data (multi-layer support) — rust_freestile
code: rust_freestile(   layers,   output_path,   tile_format,   global_min_zoom,   global_max_zoom,   base_zoom,   do_simplify,   generate_ids,   quiet,   drop_rate,   cluster_distance,   cluster_maxzoom,   do_coalesce )

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data (multi-layer support) — rust_freestile
text: layers List layer lists, containing: name, geometries, geom_types, prop_names, prop_types, prop_char_values, prop_num_values, prop_int_values, prop_lgl_values, min_zoom, max_zoom output_path Path output .pmtiles file tile_format "mvt" "mlt" global_min_zoom Minimum zoom level global_max_zoom Maximum zoom level do_simplify Whether simplify geometries lower zooms generate_ids Whether generate sequential feature IDs quiet Whether suppress progress messages drop_rate Exponential drop rate (negative = ) cluster_distance Pixel distance clustering (negative = ) cluster_maxzoom Max zoom clustering (negative = use max_zoom - 1) do_coalesce Whether coalesce features attributes

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: freestile() creates PMTiles archives sf data frames zero external dependencies (tippecanoe, Java, Go). Supports MapLibre Tiles (MLT) Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) output formats. Multi-layer output via named lists freestile_layer() per-layer zoom control.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON. Automatic CRS transformation WGS84. Z/M dimension handling (dropped automatically).

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Parallel tile encoding rayon (across tiles within tiles). Tile-pixel grid snapping zoom-adaptive simplification without slivers. Buffered tile assignment clipping seamless tile boundaries.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: drop_rate exponential feature thinning Morton-curve spatial ordering points area-based ordering polygons/lines. base_zoom control ensuring features present higher zooms. cluster_distance point clustering point_count attribute. coalesce line merging polygon grouping.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Spec-compliant MapLibre Tile encoder varint, delta, RLE, dictionary encoding. Validated mlt-core 0.1.2 reference decoder.
